title: tetratheca pilosa var. denticulata
intro: Tetratheca pilosa var. denticulata, also known as the hairy pyramid orchid, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Western Australia. It is used in traditional herbal medicine, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been observed in studies to aid in wound healing and skin conditions. The plant's medicinal properties also extend to its potential in treating various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to support the immune system. In herbal preparations, Tetratheca pilosa var. denticulata is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its availability and cultivation are strictly regulated due to its endangered status.
